Robbie and the Nuts are [YOUR AREA]'s loudest excuse for a night out — a four-piece covers band ripping through the noughties indie-rock anthems you forgot you knew every word to. Singalong choruses, a packed dance floor, and not a dull moment from first chord to last.

  • Chelsea Dagger - The Fratellis

  • Heavyweight Champion - Rev And the Makers

The Set-Up

Robbie
Singer

The gob, the front, the bloke who knows every word — and a few that were never actually written. Get him near a mic and the whole room's singing back by the second chorus, whether they meant to or not.

Edd
Guitarist

Suspiciously nimble fingers and a fret he'll never leave alone. Whatever the riff, Edd's all over it — very hands-on, slightly show-off, and frankly impossible to fret about.

Paul
Bass

Been working the low end since before half the dance floor was born. Paul's here to teach the young 'uns a lesson: the bass isn't the guitar's quiet mate, it's the engine room. Class dismissed.

Stu
Drums

A genuine unicorn — a drummer who can count past four. Stu keeps perfect time and keeps the other three in line, which makes him the most reliable thing in the band, kit included.
